reasoning: This is a fictional/conditional forecasting question (Metaculus 40437) where a hypothetical war and subsequent events occurred in 2025. According to the scenario, an IAEA inspection deal was scrapped on October 20, 2025, and there is a hard deadline of January 1, 2026 for a new agreement to be announced. The Metaculus community prediction was 6% on November 13, leaving 49 days before the deadline. As the forecast date is November 23, ten days of simulated time have passed without any apparent positive resolution, leaving only 39 days (~5.5 weeks). A time-decay adjustment of the community's 6% estimate brings the probability to approximately 4-5%. The extremely brief window remaining, the Iranian parliament's rigid stance (voting 221-0 to suspend cooperation until safety and right to enrich are guaranteed), and the recent collapse of a former deal make a breakthrough highly unlikely. Therefore, I estimate the probability at 5%.
